$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `tags` repo (Phase-2, repo #2 after
`folders`). One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness tags tier-2 test).
What this repo adds over `folders` (all-strings): a boolean column
-> compact JSON in schema field order), and the `nameLower` derivation, plus
the `delete` op. The create op carries a FULLY-specified visualStyle (all 7
fields, schema order) so no Zod inner-default expansion is involved and the
stored JSON is the input verbatim.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the folders
fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New tag with quickHide=true + a fully-specified visualStyle; id + timestamps pinned. Exercises boolean->1, object->JSON text, nameLower derivation.
Rename seed tag `Work` -> `Career` (nameLower re-derived) and flip quickHide false->true; updatedAt pinned, createdAt preserved, visualStyle stays NULL.

Non-ASCII name proves the nameLower derivation (str::to_lowercase) matches JS toLowerCase on the gnarly cases: dotted-I -> i + combining dot (0069 0307), E-acute -> e-acute, a FINAL sigma -> final-sigma form (context-sensitive), and eszett (unchanged). visualStyle null. id + timestamps pinned.
Delete seed tag `Urgent` (the one with a visualStyle object).
